 {"id":"bd-kjp","title":"Add fsqlite workspace dependency and wire up Cargo.toml","description":"Add FrankenSQLite (/dp/frankensqlite) as a workspace dependency.

## What To Do
1. In workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies], add:
   fsqlite = { path = "../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite", features = ["json"] }
   fsqlite-error = { path = "../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-error" }
   fsqlite-types = { path = "../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-types", features = ["native"] }
2. Keep the duckdb line temporarily (removed later in dedicated bead)
3. Add fsqlite to vc_store/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
4. Verify workspace resolves with cargo check

## Key Considerations
- Use path = "../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite" (sibling checkout, same pattern as asupersync)
- Enable "json" feature for JSON1 extension (needed for JSON column queries)
- FrankenSQLite is Edition 2024 (nightly), forbids unsafe_code — matches workspace
- FrankenSQLite uses asupersync (not tokio) — no new tokio deps introduced

## Files Changed
- Cargo.toml (workspace root)
- crates/vc_store/Cargo.toml","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:41.907981062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:41:51.448465598Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T18:41:51.447771589Z","close_reason":"fsqlite, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-types added as workspace deps. vc_store wired. cargo metadata resolves. Unblocks bd-bvt.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","database","fsqlite"]}
 {"id":"bd-luk","title":"Add ftui workspace dependency","description":"Add FrankenTUI (/dp/frankentui) as a workspace dependency.

## What To Do
1. In workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies], add:
   ftui = { path = "../frankentui/crates/ftui" }
2. Keep ratatui and crossterm lines temporarily (removed after all screens ported)
3. Add ftui to crates/vc_tui/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
4. Verify workspace resolves

## FrankenTUI Crate Structure
The ftui facade crate re-exports all necessary types from the underlying crates:
- ftui-core (terminal I/O, events, capabilities)
- ftui-render (buffer, diff, presenter)
- ftui-style (colors, themes, styling)
- ftui-text (spans, segments)
- ftui-layout (flex, grid constraint solvers)
- ftui-runtime (Elm event loop, Model trait)
- ftui-widgets (37+ widgets)
- ftui-harness (snapshot testing)

Only ftui needs to be in the dependency — it re-exports everything.

## Key Differences from Ratatui
- No crossterm dependency needed (ftui-core handles terminal I/O)
- Elm architecture (Model trait) instead of imperative render loop
- TerminalWriter enforces one-writer rule
- 16-byte cells instead of ratatui variable-size cells
- Inline mode is first-class, not bolted on

## Files Changed
- Cargo.toml (workspace root)
- crates/vc_tui/Cargo.toml","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:10.158783399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:14:45.197721850Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T18:14:45.193236730Z","close_reason":"ftui and ftui-harness added as workspace deps, vc_tui wired up. cargo metadata resolves clean. Unblocks bd-h8l, bd-3vb, bd-ng3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","frankentui","tui"]}
 {"id":"bd-ly5","title":"Port all vc_query unit tests to fsqlite","description":"Port all 128 vc_query unit tests from DuckDB to FrankenSQLite backend.

## Test Inventory (128 tests across 5 files)

### lib.rs — 45 tests
- Severity enum tests (serialization, ordering, round-trip)
- HealthScore/HealthFactor creation and serialization
- FleetOverview defaults and serialization
- QueryBuilder integration tests (fleet_overview, machine_health, recent_alerts, machines)
- compute_overall_score tests (empty, single, weighted, critical penalty, clamping)
- classify_metric tests (healthy/warning/critical, inverted ranges, exact thresholds)
- score_to_severity boundary tests
- HealthWeights default and lookup tests
- End-to-end scenario tests (all_healthy, disk_critical, multiple_warnings)
- persist_and_retrieve_health_score integration test
- list_health_summaries integration test

### guardrails.rs — 12 tests
- SQL validation (SELECT allowed, INSERT/DELETE/DROP rejected)
- Template expansion with integer and string params
- Unknown template handling
- SQL injection prevention
- Raw SQL disable mode
- Templates registration verification

### cost.rs — 12 tests
- Confidence factor calculation and clamping
- Provider pricing calculation
- Cost estimation for Anthropic/OpenAI models
- Unknown provider handling
- Cost attribution serialization
- In-memory store integration tests (cost_summary, list_pricing)

### digest.rs — 14 tests
- Empty database digest generation
- Weekly window digest
- Section-specific tests (fleet, alerts, collectors, events)
- Markdown rendering with tables
- Digest report storage and retrieval
- Section serialization

### nl.rs — 45 tests
- Intent classification (15 intents, 15 tests)
- Entity extraction (time range, severity, limit, machine name, search term)
- SQL generation for each intent type (with entities, limits, filters)
- NlEngine integration tests (ask unknown, machines, alerts, health)
- E2E tests (critical_alerts_last_hour, token_usage_today, top_5_machines, sessions_on_machine)

## Key Risk Areas

### INTERVAL Syntax (cost.rs, digest.rs, nl.rs — 12+ locations)
DuckDB: current_timestamp - INTERVAL '1 day'
SQLite: datetime('now', '-1 day')
Tests that generate or verify SQL strings will need updating.
nl.rs tests are highest risk — they assert on generated SQL containing "INTERVAL".

### DISTINCT ON (cost.rs — 1 location)
list_pricing test generates SQL with DISTINCT ON.
Must verify the ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY...) rewrite produces identical results.

### ILIKE (nl.rs — 3 locations)
Knowledge search tests generate SQL with ILIKE.
Must verify LIKE produces identical case-insensitive behavior for ASCII.
Note: LIKE in SQLite is case-insensitive for ASCII but case-sensitive for Unicode.

### date_trunc (nl.rs — 2 locations)
DuckDB: date_trunc('week', current_date)
SQLite: strftime('%Y-%m-%d', 'now', 'weekday 0', '-7 days')
Tests asserting exact SQL must be updated.

### current_timestamp (cost.rs, digest.rs, nl.rs — 12+ locations)
DuckDB: current_timestamp
SQLite: datetime('now')
Many tests construct or assert SQL with current_timestamp.

### to_json() in SQL (indirect via VcStore)
Some integration tests query through VcStore which uses to_json().
After bd-zut replaces to_json() with Rust-side JSON construction,
verify the test data round-trips correctly.

## What To Do
1. Run all 128 tests after vc_query SQL porting (bd-30c) is complete
2. Update nl.rs tests that assert specific SQL strings (INTERVAL, ILIKE, date_trunc)
3. Update cost.rs tests for DISTINCT ON → ROW_NUMBER rewrite
4. Fix integration tests that use VcStore::open_memory()
5. Verify all 45 nl.rs tests — these generate SQL dynamically and assert patterns
6. Verify guardrails.rs SQL validation still rejects dangerous SQL in SQLite context
7. Log per-test results: test name, file, pass/fail, fix applied

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_query/src/lib.rs (test module)
- crates/vc_query/src/cost.rs (test module)
- crates/vc_query/src/digest.rs (test module)
- crates/vc_query/src/guardrails.rs (test module)
- crates/vc_query/src/nl.rs (test module)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:08.365418917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:10:17.348011062Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["database","query","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ly5","depends_on_id":"bd-30c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:28.559990181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ly5","depends_on_id":"bd-kjk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:33:27.787723008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m76","title":"Add Semaphore-based backpressure for collector throughput","description":"Use asupersync Semaphore to limit concurrent collector operations.

## Why
With 14 collectors potentially running on multiple machines, we can overwhelm:
- SSH connection limits on remote machines
- Database write throughput
- Network bandwidth
- CPU on the monitoring machine

## Design
Global semaphore limits total concurrent collectors:

let collector_sem = asupersync::sync::Semaphore::new(MAX_CONCURRENT_COLLECTORS);

async fn run_collector(cx: &Cx, sem: &Semaphore, collector: &dyn Collector) {
    let permit = sem.acquire(cx, 1).await?;  // Blocks if at capacity
    let result = collector.collect(cx, &ctx).await;
    drop(permit);  // Release permit
    result
}

## Configuration
Add to CollectorConfig in vc_config:
- max_concurrent_collectors: u32 (default 8)
- max_concurrent_per_machine: u32 (default 4)

## Per-Machine Limits
Optionally, per-machine semaphore to prevent overwhelming a single remote host:
- machine_semaphores: HashMap<String, Semaphore>
- Each machine gets its own limit

## Cancel-Safety
Semaphore::acquire(&cx, n) is cancel-safe:
- If cancelled while waiting for permit, automatically releases
- No permit leak on cancellation

## Testing
- Unit test: acquire and release single permit — verify counter changes
- Unit test: acquire N permits where N = capacity — verify next acquire blocks
- Unit test: release permit — verify blocked acquire unblocks
- Unit test: cancel Cx while waiting for permit — verify no permit leak
- Unit test: per-machine semaphore limits 4 concurrent on same machine
- LabRuntime test: 14 collectors, semaphore capacity 8 — verify max 8 run simultaneously
- LabRuntime test: permit leak oracle — run workload, verify all permits returned
- LabRuntime test: priority scheduling — high-priority collector gets permit first (if supported)
- Integration test: real collectors with backpressure — verify throughput is bounded
- Log: permit acquired/released, queue depth, wait time, per-machine concurrency

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_collect/src/lib.rs (semaphore setup)
- crates/vc_config/src/lib.rs (configuration)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:02:04.651786372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:14:46.958174430Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","backpressure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m76","depends_on_id":"bd-3lu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:34.317808415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-mhu","title":"Port keyboard handling to ftui event system","description":"Replace crossterm event handling with ftui-core events.

## Current (crossterm)
- crossterm::event::{Event, KeyEvent, KeyCode, KeyModifiers}
- event::poll() + event::read() in main loop
- Key matching: KeyCode::Char('q'), KeyCode::Tab, etc.

## Target (ftui-core)
- ftui::core::event::{Event, KeyEvent, KeyCode, KeyEventKind, Modifiers}
- Events delivered through Model::update() as AppMessage::Key(KeyEvent)
- Key matching similar but with ftui types

## Key Mapping
| crossterm | ftui |
|-----------|------|
| KeyCode::Char(c) | KeyCode::Char(c) |
| KeyCode::Tab | KeyCode::Tab |
| KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Enter |
| KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Esc |
| KeyCode::Up/Down/Left/Right | KeyCode::Up/Down/Left/Right |
| KeyModifiers::CONTROL | Modifiers::CTRL |
| KeyModifiers::SHIFT | Modifiers::SHIFT |

## Screen Navigation Keys
Preserved from current implementation:
- 1-9, 0, b, s for screen selection
- Tab for cycling screens
- Ctrl+C or Q for quit
- Arrow keys for in-screen navigation
- Enter for selection/action

## Testing
- Unit test: each key mapping produces correct ftui KeyEvent
- Unit test: screen navigation keys (1→Overview, 2→Machines, ..., 0→Events, b→Beads, s→Settings)
- Unit test: Tab cycles screens forward
- Unit test: Shift+Tab cycles screens backward (if supported)
- Unit test: Ctrl+C produces Cmd::Quit
- Unit test: 'q' produces Cmd::Quit
- Unit test: Arrow keys produce correct navigation messages per screen
- Unit test: Enter produces selection action in list/table context
- Unit test: Esc returns to previous screen / cancels action
- Unit test: unknown key produces no state change (Cmd::None)
- Integration test: full key sequence (launch→navigate→select→back→quit)
- Log: key input, mapped event, resulting Cmd/state change

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_tui/src/lib.rs (key handling in update())","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:12.033551866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T08:01:25.292284017Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T08:01:25.291473720Z","close_reason":"Ported top-level keyboard handling to ftui events; preserved documented screen navigation, Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, Esc return-to-overview, and quit shortcuts. Screen-local arrow/Enter actions remain part of the follow-on ftui screen port beads.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","input","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mhu","depends_on_id":"bd-h8l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:45.299239792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjn","title":"Update vc_config default paths and settings for FrankenSQLite","description":"Update configuration to reflect the DuckDB -> FrankenSQLite change.

## What To Do
1. Change default_db_path() from 'vc.duckdb' to 'vc.sqlite'
   - Current: ~/.local/share/vc/vc.duckdb
   - New: ~/.local/share/vc/vc.sqlite
2. Update GlobalConfig.db_path documentation comments
3. Add optional fsqlite-specific config fields:
   - concurrent_mode: bool (default true)
   - wal_mode: bool (default true)
   - cache_size_mb: u32 (default 512)
   - max_concurrent_writers: u32 (default 16)
4. Update config linting to warn about .duckdb paths
5. Add migration hint in lint: 'db_path ends with .duckdb — did you mean .sqlite?'

## Backwards Compatibility
If user has existing config with .duckdb path:
- Config loads normally (path is just a string)
- Data migration tool (separate bead) handles file conversion
- Lint warning guides user to update path

## Testing
- Unit test: default_db_path() returns path ending in .sqlite
- Unit test: GlobalConfig with no db_path uses default (.sqlite)
- Unit test: GlobalConfig with explicit .duckdb path loads without error
- Unit test: config lint warns on .duckdb path with migration hint
- Unit test: concurrent_mode defaults to true
- Unit test: wal_mode defaults to true
- Unit test: cache_size_mb defaults to 512
- Unit test: max_concurrent_writers defaults to 16
- Unit test: custom values override defaults
- Unit test: config round-trip (serialize → deserialize preserves all fields)
- Log: config field name, default value, custom value

## Files Changed

 {"id":"bd-myy","title":"E2E test: TUI rendering verification","description":"Test that all 12 TUI screens render without errors.

## Test Scenario
Since full TUI testing requires a terminal, use ftui-harness headless mode:

1. Create App with mock data (same data as integration pipeline test)
2. For each of the 12 screens:
   a. Set current_screen to target
   b. Call Model::view() to render into Buffer
   c. Verify buffer is non-empty
   d. Verify no panic occurred
   e. Verify content includes expected elements
   f. Compare against golden snapshot (if snapshot tests exist)
3. Test navigation sequence:
   a. Start on Overview
   b. Press 1-9, 0, b, s to visit each screen
   c. Verify each transition produces correct state

## Headless Rendering
ftui Frame can render to a Buffer without a terminal:
- Create Buffer with fixed dimensions (120x40)
- Render into Buffer
- Inspect Buffer cells for content verification

## Content Verification
For each screen, verify key content:
- Overview: shows machine count > 0
- Machines: shows at least one machine row
- Accounts: shows provider names
- Sessions: shows session data
- Alerts: shows alert entries
- etc.

## Files Changed
- tests/e2e/tui_rendering.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:03:59.563990913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T12:45:11.252129055Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T12:45:11.251123485Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-myy","depends_on_id":"bd-6q0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:07:41.447331063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ohp","title":"Epic: Comprehensive Test Suite & Validation","description":"Build a comprehensive test suite that validates the entire vibe_cockpit system after the three major migrations (FrankenSQLite, FrankenTUI, deep Asupersync). This includes integration tests, E2E scripts, performance benchmarks, and data integrity verification.

## Motivation
The three concurrent migrations (database, TUI, async runtime) touch every crate in the workspace. We need thorough validation that:
- Data flows correctly through the full pipeline (Config → Collect → Store → Query → Display)
- Concurrent writes work correctly with MVCC
- TUI renders all 12 screens without visual regression
- Graceful shutdown completes within bounds
- Performance is acceptable (insert throughput, query latency)
- Existing data can be migrated from DuckDB without loss

## Test Categories
1. **Integration tests** — cross-crate data flow verification
2. **E2E scripts** — startup/shutdown, concurrent writes, TUI rendering
3. **Deterministic tests** — lab runtime scenarios for concurrency bugs
4. **Performance benchmarks** — fsqlite vs duckdb comparison
5. **Data integrity** — migration verification with checksums

## Logging Requirements
All test scripts must include:
- Timestamped step logging (INFO/DEBUG/ERROR)
- Before/after state snapshots for each operation
- Detailed error context on failure (not just "assertion failed")
- Performance timing for each test step
- Summary report at end with pass/fail counts

## Success Criteria
- 100% of existing tests pass after migration
- E2E scripts cover all critical paths
- Lab tests reproduce and verify known concurrency scenarios
- Performance regression < 20% vs DuckDB for typical queries
- Data migration verified with row count and checksum matching","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:06.324763134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:39:53.313655271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-9tig","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:39:53.312882984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-axq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:11:54.122270136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-f2q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:12:26.733718119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-hdic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:13:15.622312528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-myy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:12:43.121207690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-spi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:12:59.393683413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ohp","depends_on_id":"bd-zxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:12:11.009924541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-oy2","title":"Phase 3: Migrate all test suites to Asupersync determinism","notes":"Background: All crates currently use #[tokio::test]. We must fully eliminate Tokio from the test environment to guarantee deterministic behavior.

Tasks:
1. Strip #[tokio::test] from all modules (vc_query, vc_store, vc_oracle, vc_cli, vc_web, etc.).
2. Replace them with Asupersync's test runner macro or manual asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new().block_on() blocks.
3. Utilize Asupersync's deterministic test labs (src/lab) to rewrite the most critical concurrent tests (e.g., the adaptive scheduler in vc_collect, channel operations in vc_alert), verifying mathematically that no deadlocks or race conditions exist.

Testing & Validation:
- Unit Tests: Ensure coverage remains at or above current levels. All 1,200+ tests must pass under the Asupersync runtime.
- E2E Tests: Implement e2e_determinism_check.sh to run the test suite multiple times under varying CPU loads to ensure test flakes are eradicated.
- Logging: Enhance test output logging so that if an Asupersync deterministic lab test fails, it prints the exact thread interleaving and scheduling decisions that led to the panic/deadlock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.465965811Z","created_by":"unknown","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:42:27.914844636Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:37.005416970Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-125","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.524040490Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-1a4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.193664177Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.842597864Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-34e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:01:26.408117293Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-35x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.872400460Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-oy2","author":"codex","text":"Progress update: removed all remaining #[tokio::test] markers across the workspace and converted the affected vc_collect/vc_web tests to Asupersync-rooted harnesses.  {"id":"bd-phr","title":"Translate migrations 010-018 to SQLite-compatible SQL","description":"Port migrations 10-18 from DuckDB syntax to SQLite syntax.

## Migrations to Port (202 lines total, medium complexity)
10. 010_retention_log.sql (20 lines) — Data retention tracking, vacuum logs
11. 011_collector_health.sql (56 lines) — Collector run health/status tracking, 6 indexes
12. 012_health_scores.sql (12 lines) — ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN to health_summary (3 columns)
13. 013_alert_delivery_log.sql (14 lines) — Alert delivery attempt tracking
14. 014_autopilot_decisions.sql (13 lines) — Autonomous remediation decision log
15. 015_incident_notes.sql (15 lines) — Human/agent notes on incidents, 4 indexes (3 on tables from 001)
16. 016_fleet_commands.sql (18 lines) — Fleet-wide command execution tracking, 3 indexes
17. 017_mined_sessions.sql (15 lines) — Sessions mined for knowledge extraction, 2 indexes
18. 018_playbook_autogen.sql (38 lines) — Auto-generated playbook templates, 3 indexes

## Translation Rules (same as bd-dfl)
- TIMESTAMP -> TEXT (ISO8601 strings)
- BOOLEAN -> INTEGER (0/1)
- BIGINT -> INTEGER
- DOUBLE -> REAL
- current_timestamp -> datetime('now')

## Special Considerations
- 012 uses ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN — SQLite supports this (one column per ALTER)
  - If original has multiple ADD COLUMN in one ALTER, split into separate statements
- 015 adds indexes on tables from migration 001 (incidents, incident_timeline)
  - These are cross-migration index additions — valid in SQLite
- 19 CREATE INDEX statements across this batch — all use IF NOT EXISTS
  - SQLite CREATE INDEX syntax is identical, should work as-is
- No array types, no ENUM types, no JSON types in this batch (simpler than 001-009)
- No foreign keys in this batch

## Testing
After translation, verify:
1. Each migration file parses in fsqlite
2. Tables created with correct column types
3. ALTER TABLE statements apply cleanly
4. All 19 indexes created successfully
5. INSERT test data accepted

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_store/src/migrations/010_retention_log.sql through 018_playbook_autogen.sql","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.093921436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:17:10.299898659Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T06:17:10.299231159Z","close_reason":"Translated migrations 010-018 to SQLite-compatible SQL; verified with sqlite3 parse/apply and insert smoke tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["database","migrations","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-phr","depends_on_id":"bd-axj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:04.255202068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-phr","depends_on_id":"bd-dfl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:33:15.118860698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-qdp","title":"Implement graceful shutdown with bounded drain","description":"Use asupersync budgets and regions for guaranteed bounded shutdown.

## Current State
Shutdown is a hard timeout: if collectors don't stop within N seconds, process exits.
This can leave SSH connections open, partial data in the database.

## Target Architecture
Use asupersync budget deadlines on the collector region:

async fn run_with_shutdown(cx: &mut Cx) {
    let shutdown_budget = Budget {
        deadline: Some(now_ms() + 30_000),
        ..Default::default()
    };

    cx.region_with_budget(shutdown_budget, |scope| async move {
        scope.spawn(collector_fleet);
        scope.spawn(web_server);
        scope.spawn(alert_engine);

        // Wait for shutdown signal
        cx.checkpoint().await;  // Blocks until Ctrl+C

        // Region close: cancels all children, drains within budget
    }).await;
    // Guaranteed: everything stopped within 30 seconds
}

## What To Do
1. Wrap main application in a top-level region
2. Set budget deadline for shutdown (configurable, default 30s)
3. Handle Ctrl+C signal via asupersync::signal::ctrl_c()
4. On signal: cancel the region (propagates to all children)
5. Region close drives drain phase:
   - Collectors hit checkpoints and start cleanup
   - SSH connections close
   - Database transactions commit or rollback
   - Web server stops accepting connections, drains active requests
6. If budget expires: force-stop remaining tasks
7. Log drain progress: 'Shutting down: 12/14 collectors drained'

## Observable Drain
Use tracing to report:
- Total children in region
- Children completed vs still draining
- Budget remaining
- Any tasks force-stopped at deadline

## Testing
- Unit test: region shutdown with all children completing before deadline — clean exit
- Unit test: region shutdown with one slow child exceeding deadline — force-stopped
- Unit test: budget of 0ms — immediate force-stop of all children
- Unit test: Ctrl+C signal triggers region cancellation
- Integration test: start daemon with mock collectors, send SIGINT, verify exit within 35 seconds
- Integration test: verify database transactions committed (no partial writes after shutdown)
- Integration test: verify SSH connections closed (no leaked connections after shutdown)
- Test drain progress logging: verify "Shutting down: N/M collectors drained" messages appear
- LabRuntime test: deterministic shutdown sequence with virtual time
- Log: shutdown signal time, drain start time, per-child drain status, budget remaining, exit code

## Files Changed
- src/main.rs (top-level region)
- crates/vc_cli/src/lib.rs (shutdown handling)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:02:03.357535848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:12:19.781578994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","shutdown"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qdp","depends_on_id":"bd-3lu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:29.708235932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-r5y","title":"Epic: DuckDB → FrankenSQLite Migration","description":"Complete replacement of DuckDB with FrankenSQLite as the storage backend for Vibe Cockpit.

## Motivation
DuckDB's bundled builds consistently OOM on remote compilation workers (exit 137) and locally (SIGKILL). FrankenSQLite (/dp/frankensqlite) is a ground-up Rust reimplementation of SQLite 3.52.0 that provides:
- **MVCC concurrent writers** (page-level locking, not file-level) — enables parallel collector inserts without contention
- **Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI)** — prevents write skew in concurrent mode
- **Safe Rust engine** (forbid(unsafe_code) in core) — matches our workspace policy
- **Asupersync-native** — no tokio dependency, uses Cx capability tokens throughout
- **MigrationRunner** built-in — replaces our custom migration system
- **RaptorQ self-healing storage** — information-theoretic durability
- **No OOM builds** — pure Rust, no bundled C compilation

## Scope
- Port VcStore from duckdb::Connection to fsqlite::Connection
- Translate all 26 DuckDB migrations to SQLite-compatible SQL
- Port all query methods (handle DuckDB-specific functions like to_json(), LIST types, etc.)
- Port vc_knowledge's direct DuckDB usage
- Enable MVCC concurrent_mode for multi-collector writes with conflict retry
- Update vc_config paths and error types workspace-wide
- Build data migration tool for existing .duckdb files
- Port all unit tests with behavioral parity

## Key Technical Differences
- DuckDB LIST/MAP/STRUCT types → JSON TEXT columns in SQLite
- DuckDB to_json() → manual JSON construction via serde
- DuckDB TIMESTAMP → TEXT (ISO8601) in SQLite
- DuckDB BOOLEAN → INTEGER (0/1) in SQLite
- DuckDB parameterized ? → SQLite ?N or ?
- DuckDB execute_batch() → fsqlite execute() (multi-statement)
- DuckDB analytical functions → SQLite window functions + JSON1
- Memory limit pragma → cache_size pragma
- Thread count pragma → not needed (single-process, MVCC handles concurrency)

## Success Criteria
- Zero DuckDB references in workspace (Cargo.toml, use statements, error types)
- All existing tests pass with FrankenSQLite backend
- Concurrent collector writes verified with MVCC
- Build succeeds on RCH workers without OOM
- Data migration tool handles existing installations","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:16.054987590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T04:09:12.341729463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["database","fsqlite","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-1kc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:08:27.437821108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-9us","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:08:49.513946759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-a38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:08:06.160943840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-f9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:09:12.341052115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-kjk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:08:16.741671663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-r5y","depends_on_id":"bd-ly5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:08:38.226016958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-rf7","title":"Implement bv + br (Beads) collector","description":"Implement collector for bv (Beads Viewer) and br (Beads Rust) - task tracking and productivity metrics.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- bv --robot-triage for mega-command JSON output
- br ready --json for actionable work
- br sync --flush-only to export JSONL, then read .beads/issues.jsonl
- Use CLI Snapshot pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE beads_triage_snapshots(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    repo_id TEXT,
    quick_ref_json TEXT,
    recommendations_json TEXT,
    project_health_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_issues(
    repo_id TEXT,
    issue_id TEXT,
    status TEXT,
    priority TEXT,
    type TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    labels_json TEXT,
    deps_json TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, issue_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_graph_metrics(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    pagerank_json TEXT,
    betweenness_json TEXT,
    critical_path_json TEXT,
    cycles_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement BeadsCollector with Collector trait
- Run bv --robot-triage in each repo with .beads directory
- Parse triage output for quick_ref, recommendations, health
- Store graph metrics (pagerank, betweenness, critical path)
- Handle missing bv/br binaries gracefully

REPO DISCOVERY:
- Get repo list from ru collector data
- Check each repo for .beads directory
- Only collect from repos with beads initialized

UI VALUE:
- What to work on next across all projects
- Project health at a glance
- Bottleneck identification
- Dependency visualization

TESTS:
- Create test .beads databases
- Mock bv --robot-triage output
- Verify issue synchronization

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-rq9","title":"Port Alerts, Guardian, and Oracle screens to ftui","description":"Port Alerts, Guardian, and Oracle screens from ratatui to FrankenTUI widgets.

## Alerts Screen (screens/alerts.rs, 643 lines)
Current: Alert history with severity, message, timestamps, acknowledgment status.
Uses: Paragraph, Block, Span, Style

Port to ftui:
- ftui Table for alert list (severity icon, message, timestamp, status)
- ftui Badge for severity (critical=red, warning=yellow, info=blue)
- ftui Badge for acknowledgment status (open/acked/resolved)
- Color coding: severity_indicator from widgets.rs
- Filtering support: by severity, by timeframe

## Guardian Screen (screens/guardian.rs, 731 lines)
Current: Self-healing engine dashboard with playbooks, runs, approval queue.
Uses: Paragraph, Block, Span, Style

Port to ftui:
- ftui Tabs for switching between playbooks/runs/approvals views
- ftui Table for playbook list (name, trigger, last run, status)
- ftui List for approval queue (pending items)
- ftui ProgressBar for run progress
- ftui Badge for run status (success/failed/running/pending_approval)

## Oracle Screen (screens/oracle.rs, 820 lines)
Current: Prediction engine showing forecasts, confidence, recommendations.
Uses: Paragraph, Block, Span, Style

Port to ftui:
- ftui Table for prediction list (metric, forecast, confidence, recommendation)
- ftui Sparkline for trend visualization per metric
- ftui ProgressBar for confidence level
- Color coding: confidence-based coloring (high=green, low=red)

## Testing
- Render Alerts with mixed severity (critical, warning, info) — verify color coding
- Render Alerts with acknowledged/resolved states — verify badge display
- Render Alerts empty — verify "no alerts" message
- Render Guardian with playbooks and approval queue — verify tab navigation
- Render Guardian with pending approvals — verify approval items visible
- Render Guardian empty — verify "no playbooks configured" message
- Render Oracle with predictions — verify confidence bars and colors
- Render Oracle with Sparkline trends — verify no rendering errors
- Render Oracle empty — verify "no predictions available" message
- Snapshot test: each screen normal and empty state (6 golden files)
- Log: screen name, data size, render time

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_tui/src/screens/alerts.rs
- crates/vc_tui/src/screens/guardian.rs
- crates/vc_tui/src/screens/oracle.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:00.413113439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T11:15:19.104554089Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T11:15:19.103924262Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","screens","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rq9","depends_on_id":"bd-h8l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:59.107808296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rq9","depends_on_id":"bd-mhu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:33:18.824437698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rq9","depends_on_id":"bd-ng3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:57.214377635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
